title: Campanula buseri
taxonomy: Campanula buseri is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Campanulaceae. This plant is further categorized in the order Asterales. At the family level, it is a member of the Campanulaceae family, commonly known as bellflowers. Campanula buseri belongs to the genus Campanula, which comprises a wide variety of flowering plants. Its specific classification is Campanula buseri, a species within the Campanula genus.

side_effects: Campanula buseri, being a plant, typically does not have direct side effects on humans as it's not commonly used as a medication. intro: Campanula buseri, also known as Bellflower, is a perennial plant species native to the European Alps, known for its bell-shaped flowers and medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Campanula buseri include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Campanula buseri include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues and relieve pain.
